data.title: 'Stay Away,' Say Strategists About VCX
data.description:
Fundrise Growth Tech Fund (VCX) has surged over 200% over the past few days following its recent NYSE debut, and there are still no signs of cooling off, with the stock climbing in double digits again today.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The move is being driven by what sits inside the portfolio. VCX holds stakes in some of the most sought-after private technology companies, including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Databricks, which together form a core part of today's AI ecosystem. The fund also includes names like SpaceX, Anduril, and Ramp, giving investors exposure across multiple layers of the AI and technology stack that are typically inaccessible through public markets.

For many investors, VCX offers a rare opportunity to gain indirect exposure to private AI leaders without needing to participate in venture capital rounds, which helps explain why buyers have continued stepping in even after such a steep move. Furthermore, VCX behaves more like a closed-end fund than a traditional ETF, meaning the share price can move well above the underlying net asset value when demand outpaces supply.

Still, several strategists are urging caution at these levels.

"It's great for investors who want to get out. But if you are coming in, you are paying a huge, huge premium for the NAV," said Matt Malone, the head of investment management at Opto Investments.

Jack Shannon, equity strategies principal at Morningstar, also questioned whether the current pricing reflects any rational benchmark: "With the implied valuations when you have this premium, your upside is gone. Clearly, it's going to attract some meme crowd and get some high octane trading. But if someone is in this for the long term, frankly, it's a horrible investment at the current price."
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Arm Holdings Stock Skyrockets over 11% as Guggenheim Gives ARM Stock the Most Bullish Forecast with 78% Upside
data.description:
ARM Holdings (ARM) stock skyrocketed more than 11% in pre-market trading this morning, Wednesday, March 25, 2026, as Wall Street reacts to the company's shift into making its own hardware. John DiFucci, the Senior Managing Director at Guggenheim, officially raised his price target for ARM from $201 to $240. This new target suggests a huge 77.83% upside, making it one of the most bullish calls on the market this morning.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

New AGI Chip Supports Massive Revenue Goals

The upgrade follows ARM's "Arm Everywhere" event in San Francisco, where the company announced its first-ever in-house processor called the AGI CPU. This is a huge shift for ARM, which usually just licenses its designs to other companies. In his direct report released this morning, DiFucci explained that "the 'Arm Everywhere' initiative represents a fundamental shift in the company's DNA, moving from a passive licensor to an active participant in the AI compute value chain."

Guggenheim's report highlights that this new business could generate $15 billion in annual revenue within the next five years. DiFucci noted that ARM is one of only three companies in his coverage that will see clear and direct benefits from the rise of artificial intelligence. By selling its own chips directly to giants like Meta and OpenAI, ARM is moving from being a designer to a major hardware player.

ARM Brings Powerful Performance to Agentic AI

The report from Guggenheim also focuses on the technical edge of the new AGI CPU. This chip is specifically designed for "agentic AI" workloads, which means it helps AI programs think and act on their own. DiFucci wrote in his report that "the launch of the AGI CPU is not just a new product; it is the cornerstone of a strategy that we expect to capture $15 billion in incremental revenue by 2031."

The chip delivers twice the performance of comparable platforms while using much less power. This is a major advantage for companies like Meta, which is the flagship customer for the new silicon. DiFucci believes that among the names his firm covers, "ARM remains one of the few with a direct, unencumbered path to benefit from the 'agentic AI' revolution."

Analyst Remains Prudent as Earnings Skyrocket

Even with the huge price target increase, DiFucci's report says he is taking a "prudent approach" to the company's new long-term goals. While the $25 billion total revenue target for 2031 is ambitious, the early momentum with customers gives him confidence.

The report also highlights ARM's incredible 97.5% gross profit margin, which is one of the highest in the entire semiconductor industry. This financial strength supports the company's plan to spend more on research and development as it builds its own chips. DiFucci expects earnings per share to eventually reach above $9, a massive jump from previous forecasts.

Is Arm Stock a Strong Buy?

Yes, Arm Holdings stock is indeed a Strong Buy, according to TipRanks. This is based on 20 Buy, four Hold and one Sell recommendation. The average 12-month ARM stock price target of $165.52 indicates 22.65% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Dow Jones Rallies on U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Optimism
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Wednesday in the green as investors monitor the latest developments in the U.S.-Iran war.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

The U.S. has sent Iran a list of 15 demands to end the war, including ending uranium enrichment, pledging to never pursue nuclear weapons, and reopening the Strait of Hormuz as a free maritime corridor. Iran has also provided its own demands, pushing for a public apology from President Trump to Mojtaba Khamenei, recognition of its nuclear rights, and the evacuation of all U.S. bases from the Gulf.

U.S., Iran Refuse Talks as Tensions Rise

However, both sides declined to meet for a first round of discussions this week, with a top U.S. official labeling Iran's demands as "ridiculous and unrealistic." Iran has remained defiant as well, with IRGC spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaghari saying, "Someone like us will never get along with someone like you. Not now, not ever."

Iran has also rejected the possibility of a ceasefire and said that peace talks are not viable under current conditions, according to the country's Fars News Agency.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why is VolitionRX Stock (VNRX) Up 24% Today?
data.description:
Shares in VolitionRx (VNRX) surged 24% in pre-market trading today as it hailed a breakthrough in the detection of early-stage cancers.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Noteworthy Trial Results

The epigenetics company said that a trial utilizing its Capture-Seq technology involving 81 colorectal and lung cancer patients had created "compelling proof of concept data."

The company said: "Volition has developed a new liquid biopsy method, Capture-Seq, which enriches and purifies plasma samples resulting in virtually pure circulating tumor DNA samples for analysis. We are extremely encouraged by the results, particularly in early-stage cancer detection of 95% of stage I and II cancers. That is noteworthy."

Dr. Andrew Retter, Medical Consultant, Volition, added: "For patients, the potential significance is huge. If validated in larger cohorts, CTCF Capture-Seq could contribute to Multi-Cancer Early Detection fulfilling a significant unmet clinical need. We also believe Capture-Seq has the potential to play a role in cancer management, including, but not limited to, Minimal Residual Disease (MRD) detection and treatment monitoring, either alone or potentially in combination with other technologies too."

Pet and Human Detection

Volition believes that the tech could, with further development, become very widely used, in both the human and veterinary markets.

 "We believe this represents a significant commercial opportunity with a Total Addressable Market on an annualized basis of approximately $23 billion for the human MCED use, and over $13 billion for MRD," added Gael Forterre, Chief Commercial Officer, Volition. "We are in active discussions with several large liquid biopsy and diagnostic companies to accelerate the development and launch of this technology as soon as possible."

Is VNRX a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Given the lack of Wall Street analysts covering this stock, let's look at how the VNRX share price has performed over the last three months. As can be seen below, it is down around 20%.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: We're Saying Goodbye to Sora": OpenAI Shuts Down Video App as Disney (DIS) Exits Deal
data.description:
OpenAI is preparing to shut down its Sora AI video app, and the move is already affecting one of its biggest partners in Disney (DIS). The change points to a shift in how the company plans to use its video tools, while also raising questions about how media firms will work with AI going forward.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

OpenAI confirmed the change on X, stating, "We're saying goodbye to Sora… What you made with Sora mattered, and we know this news is disappointing." The company added that it will share more details soon, including timing for the app and API, as well as options for users to keep their work. It appears that the Sora app, which drew wide attention for its high-quality video clips, is not moving ahead as a standalone product.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Merck Chases $6B Terns Pharma Deal Ahead of Keytruda Patent Expiry — TERN and MRK Stocks Rise
data.description:
U.S.-based Merck & Co. (MRK) is reportedly nearing a $6 billion all-cash deal to acquire biotech company Terns Pharmaceuticals (TERN) as part of its plan to strengthen its drug pipeline ahead of Keytruda's patent expiration in 2028. Notably, Keytruda (pembrolizumab) is Merck's blockbuster immunotherapy drug and one of the world's best-selling medicines, widely used to treat various types of cancers. The Financial Times reported that the talks are at an advanced stage and could close in the coming days. TERN stock is up 5% pre-market after deal report, MRK edges up 0.41%.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Merck develops medicines, vaccines, and biologics, with a focus on oncology and hospital products. Meanwhile, Terns Pharmaceuticals is a biotech company working on new medicines for liver disease (NASH), obesity, and cancer.

Merck Aims to Protect Oncology Revenue

Merck leads the global oncology segment and is best known for Keytruda, which drives nearly half of the company's sales. In 2025, Keytruda generated around $31.7 billion in sales, marking 7% year-over-year growth. However, the latest report indicates that Keytruda could face loss of exclusivity as early as 2028.

To prepare for the upcoming Keytruda patent expiration, Merck is pursuing deals to strengthen and diversify its cancer drug pipeline, with CEO Rob Davis leading the strategy. The potential acquisition of Terns Pharmaceuticals is the latest step in this plan. Terns' lead drug targets chronic myeloid leukemia (CML), a rare cancer affecting the blood and bone marrow. By adding such early-stage assets, Merck aims to bolster its oncology pipeline and ensure long-term growth.

Last month, the company announced that it will create a separate division for its cancer business, centered on its top-selling drug Keytruda to prepare for the upcoming patent expiration.

Neither Merck nor Terns Pharma has officially commented on the acquisition reports.

Is MRK Stock a Good Buy Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on MRK stock based on 13 Buys and six Holds assigned in the past three months. The average Merck price target of $131.42 per share implies a 13% upside potential.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Spotify (SPOT) Cracks Down on AI Imposters Hijacking Real Artists' Profiles
data.description:
Spotify (SPOT), the popular music streaming app, is currently conducting a beta test for a new feature called "Artist Profile Protection." This tool will enable artists to review their tracks before they appear on their profiles. This move is aimed at reducing the occurrence of AI-generated and incorrect tracks being attributed to the wrong artist.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Spotify Introduces Artist Profile Protection

Spotify's new Artist Profile Protection feature lets artists review releases before they go live on their profiles. Only tracks approved by the artist will appear on their profile, count toward their stats, and be included in user recommendations. The company has explained that music appearing on the wrong artist pages across streaming services is now more common, particularly due to AI-generated music.

"That's not the experience we want artists to have on Spotify, and that's why we've made protecting artist identity a top priority for 2026," Spotify said in a blog post.

Notably, AI music is becoming more prevalent on streaming services. Recently, Sony Group (SONY) announced that it had requested the deletion of over 135,000 songs impersonating its artists. These problems can arise from metadata issues, confusion between artists with similar names, or deliberate attempts to associate songs with the wrong artist profiles. When this happens, it can affect an artist's catalog, distort performance stats, and change how listeners discover their music.

How the New Feature Works

The new profile protection feature is designed for artists who have experienced repeated issues with incorrect releases or those with common names. Artists in the beta can access it through their "Spotify for Artists" settings on desktop and mobile web. 

Once turned on, they receive email notifications whenever a release is submitted under their name and can approve or decline it before it appears on their profile. Notably, Spotify has described its new artist profile protection tool as a "first-of-its-kind solution to a problem that's affected streaming for years."

Is Spotify a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks' analyst forecast data, Spotify (SPOT) currently has a "Strong Buy" consensus, with an average price target of $653, indicating solid upside potential of about 34%. The outlook suggests confidence in the company's music streaming capabilities and its continued growth among competitors. Investors can use TipRanks' Stocks Comparison Tool to check ratings, price targets, and performance for SPOT and other tech and music stocks.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Braze Stock (BRZE) Surges Today Despite Mixed Q4 Earnings Results
data.description:
Braze (BRZE) stock surged on Wednesday following the release of the customer engagement platform company's Fiscal Q4 2026 earnings report. The highlights from this report included adjusted earnings per share of 10 cents and revenue of $205.2 million. While the company's adjusted EPS was below Wall Street's estimate of 14 cents, its revenue was above Wall Street's estimate of $198.24 million. Investors will also note that EPS dipped from the 12 cents reported in Fiscal Q4 2025, while revenue grew year-over-year from $160.4 million.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Braze reported a widened operating loss in Q4 2026, which it largely attributed to $37.3 million of stock-based compensation expense. However, the company noted that revenue grew on new customers, upsells, and renewals. This also led to a strong increase in subscription revenue to $193.5 million from $153.9 million in the same period of the previous year.

Braze also announced a share repurchase program in its latest earnings report. The company will repurchase up to $100 million of BRZE stock. As part of this program, the company intends to enter into an accelerated share repurchase transaction for $50 million worth of BRZE stock.

Braze Provides New Guidance

Braze provided investors with a guidance update for its Fiscal first quarter and full year of 2027 in its latest earnings report. The company expects:

  • Revenue of $204.5 million to $205.5 million in Q1 and $884 million to $889 million in 2027.
  • Non-GAAP operating income of $10 to $11 million in Q1 and $69 million to $73 million in 2027.
  • Non-GAAP net income of $11 million to $12 million in Q1 and $69 million to $73 million in 2027.
  • Diluted Non-GAAP net income per share of 10 cents to 11 cents in Q1 and 61 cents to 65 cents in 2027.

BRZE Stock Movement Today

Braze stock was up 20.31% in pre-market trading on Wednesday, following a 4.76% fall yesterday. The stock has fallen 47.45% year-to-date and 51.73% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Futures Rise on News of a Peace Plan to Resolve the U.S.-Iran Conflict
data.description:
Stock futures trended higher on Wednesday morning after the New York Times reported that the U.S. had sent a 15-point peace plan to Iran. Oil prices fell sharply amid rising hopes for the de-escalation of tensions in the Middle East. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.71%, 0.55%, and 0.58%, respectively, at 8:35 a.m. EST on March 25.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

At the time of writing, the international oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was down 3.85% to $100.61 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) fell 3.67% to trade at $88.88 per barrel.

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower, reversing earlier gains from President Trump's claims of "productive conversations." Markets dipped after Iranian officials denied direct peace talks with the U.S. The Dow fell 0.18%, the Nasdaq declined 0.84%, and the S&P 500 shed 0.37%.

Turning to trending stocks, shares of Arm Holdings (ARM) rallied 12% in Wednesday's pre-market trading after the chipmaker unveiled its first in-house chip, saying it would generate $15 billion in revenue by 2031. Terns Pharmaceuticals (TERN) jumped 5% after Merck (MRK) agreed to buy the biopharma company in a deal valued at $6.7 billion.

Robinhood Markets (HOOD) was up 3% after the financial services platform announced a $1.5 billion stock buyback plan. Also, Chewy (CHWY) stock rallied more than 10% after the pet supplies retailer announced a solid revenue outlook.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju
